---
title: 'Everyone has a story: Amos the Engineer'
date: '2015-11-07T22:20:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4266988707378364778'
blogger_filename: '/2015/11/everyone-has-story-amos-engineer.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Stories'
---

<br /><br /><h4>Amos the Engineer</h4><br />Walking through Walmart is an interesting way to meet people. As I approach the Walmart Vision Center, there is an older man in a lab coat, looking down at his paperwork. He sits alone, in the back of the vision center, at a desk behind a computer.<br /><br />It's a cool rainy Sunday evening, about 7pm, not many people in the store tonight. I walk in, and head straight for the Men's glasses wall. I already know what I'm looking for, generally. I want glasses with built in removable sunglass, that clip on through magnets. I've had a paid for years, and I want a new pair. Surprisingly, these are hard to find this year. Most places do not carry them, and didn't know about them. No surprisingly, Walmart has two rows of them from two different companies. Hurrah!<br /><br />The gentlemen stands up and approaches. The first thing I notice, besides his white lab coat, is how tall he is. He's well over 6' tall, I have to bend my head backward to look up at him. He's definitely older, not sure how old (70's?). His name tag says Amos.<br /><br />He proceeds to tell me about the Walmart way of doing glasses, the warranties, the time frames, the costs. Then he starts to explain the individual components and features of the two brands. And this is when my world changed for an hour. He was fascinating. It could be the that the input and learner sides of my personality were kicking into high gear, but I was engrossed.<br /><br />He handed me a pair of glasses with a particularly interesting feature. The Hinge is built in like a socket, and can move 360*. The <a href="http://www.aspexeyewear.com/en/seeproduct.php?model=EC341" target="_blank">EasyClip</a>'s are my favorite.<br /><br /><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/easyclip-ec341-90-b-27e02d3220cf.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="182" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/easyclip-ec341-90-b-11eb52db5cd6.jpg" width="400" /></a></div><br /><br />Amos proceeded with his own level of enjoyment and fascination, to explain the hinge system and how it works, the materials they were made out of, the process through which they make the special lenses.<br /><br />We settled on a pair for him to write up, and sat down at the desk. I was enjoying his passion for the topic. I probed and asked questions. In 1964, Amos was one of the chief engineers that helped develop the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raised_pavement_marker" target="_blank">Raised Pavement Markers</a> we use in the US today. Of course the patents went to the people funding the research. He went on to start his own engineering company, helped develop the standards for Auto Manufacturers lighting equipment (think headlights and tail lights).<br /><br />After he retired, he didn't want to sit on his butt all day, so he came to work part time for Walmart, using his passion for engineering to discuss eye glasses.<br /><br /><br /><h4>This got me thinking... everyone has a story</h4><br />Some of the best lessons I've learned in life were from people I met in the course of my day (work, shopping, etc). What do to, not to do; what works and doesn't; etc...<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>A smart man makes a mistake, learns from it, and never makes that mistake again. BUT, a wise man finds a smart man and learns from him how to avoid the mistake altogether.&nbsp;</i></blockquote><br /><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/d11lsn3axbj16p-cloudfront-net/1446933434-a317260a-b286-15bba6831585.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="248" src="/images/imported/d11lsn3axbj16p-cloudfront-net/1446933434-a317260a-b286-15bba6831585.jpg" width="320" /></a></div><br /><br /><br />I don't know that I learned any mistakes from Amos, but I learned a lot about glasses, which ones will work best for me. I learned that there is a lot more to the person you pass by than first meets the eye.<br /><br /><br /><h4>Your Turn: What's your story?</h4><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">Tell the LIFE community about a story you encountered this week, and what you learned from it.&nbsp;</blockquote><h4><div style="font-weight: normal;"><b>Comment on this post, or Hit me up on Twitter&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/darrellwolfe" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe&nbsp;</a>,&nbsp;Google&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank">+DarrellWolfe</a>, Facebook&nbsp;<a href="https://www.facebook.com/groups/907869989282584/" target="_blank">DarrellGWolfe</a>,&nbsp;</b></div></h4><b><br /></b><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>Tweet These:</b><br />#<a href="http://ctt.ec/8E5X7" target="_blank">TellYourStory</a>#<a href="http://ctt.ec/5xc67" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a></blockquote><br /><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Storyteller, Creative,&nbsp;<a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" target="_blank">INFJ</a>,&nbsp;<a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder" target="_blank">Intellection</a>, Ideation, Input, Learner, Achiever</i></blockquote><i><br /></i><hr color="black" noshade="" /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><br /><img border="0" height="232" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" width="320" /></a></div><div><br /><div><ul><li><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">New Here? Start HERE</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/got-freedom.html" target="_blank">Got LIFE?</a></span></li><li><a href="http://eepurl.com/MzAVr" target="_blank">Sign up for email delivery of these posts here</a>.&nbsp;</li></ul><ul><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/blog-page_27.html" target="_blank">Links to Resources</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/my-tool-box.html" target="_blank">My Tool Box</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" target="_blank">Book Suggestions</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/banking-basics-for-believers-book-by.html" target="_blank">Click Here To Get My Book: "Banking Basics for Believers"</a>, which guides you through the basics of banking and money from a Christian Perspective.&nbsp;</li></ul><div></div><div></div></div><div><b>For more book suggestions, see my Amazon Store:</b></div><div><br /></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="http://astore.amazon.com/your0f4d-20" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;" target="_blank"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/amazon-button-8fb3d7b755c7.gif" /></a></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><b>Other Hobby Sties by Darrell:</b><br /><br /><ul><li><a href="http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com</a></li><ul><li>End Times Prophecy Study</li></ul><li><a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">http://authorfun.blogspot.com</a></li><ul><li>Fiction and Flash Fiction Practice</li></ul><li><a href="http://www.lifeinfortworth.com/" target="_blank">http://www.lifeinfortworth.com</a></li><ul><li>Discover, Navigate, and Enjoy Fort Worth Texas. Reviews</li></ul></ul></div><div><ul><ul></ul></ul></div><div><ul><ul></ul></ul></div>
